techniques.evidence_quote: Uber has shared that threat actors stole its data in a recent breach on Teqtivity, which it uses for asset management and tracking services.
a threat actor gained access to a Teqtivity AWS backup server that stores data for its customers.
a threat actor named 'UberLeaks' began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum
began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum known for publishing data breaches.

attack_chain.description: Threat actor gained unauthorized access to Teqtivity's systems, a trusted third-party vendor providing asset management services to Uber and other customers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: customer data that was compromised due to unauthorized access to our systems by a malicious third party

evidence_chunks.quote: customer data that was compromised due to unauthorized access to our systems by a malicious third party
customer data that was compromised due to unauthorized access to our systems by a malicious third party

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed a Teqtivity AWS backup server containing code and customer data files including device information and user information.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The third party was able to gain access to our Teqtivity AWS backup server that housed Teqtivity code and data files related to Teqtivity customers.

evidence_chunks.quote: The third party was able to gain access to our Teqtivity AWS backup server that housed Teqtivity code and data files related to Teqtivity customers.
The third party was able to gain access to our Teqtivity AWS backup server that housed Teqtivity code and data files related to Teqtivity customers.

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated source code, IT asset management reports, data destruction reports, Windows domain login names, and email addresses for over 77,000 Uber employees.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the newly leaked data consists of source code, IT asset management reports, data destruction reports, Windows domain login names and email addresses

evidence_chunks.quote: the newly leaked data consists of source code, IT asset management reports, data destruction reports, Windows domain login names and email addresses
the newly leaked data consists of source code, IT asset management reports, data destruction reports, Windows domain login names and email addresses

attack_chain.description: Threat actor 'UberLeaks' publicly leaked the stolen Uber/Uber Eats/Teqtivity data on a hacking forum known for data breaches.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a threat actor named 'UberLeaks' began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum

evidence_chunks.quote: a threat actor named 'UberLeaks' began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum
a threat actor named 'UberLeaks' began leaking data they claimed was stolen from Uber and Uber Eats on a hacking forum

initial_access_vector: Unknown root cause; unauthorized third-party access to Teqtivity's AWS backup server. Affected downstream customer (Uber) via trusted third-party vendor relationship.
